project.name: Ableton Project Setup
project.description: Automated Ableton Live project builder — takes raw stems, classifies them, detects BPM, and creates a fully laid-out .als file matching Sam's mixing workflow.
project.location: Wired Masters Dropbox/Sam Wills/0.1---GIT HUB---/Ableton Project Setup/

sessions.summary: Project bootstrap — created context files, reviewed ABLETON_INTERACTION.md from Automated DJ Mixes. Waiting for Sam's reference ALS files to analyse his mix layout.
pending: Receive and analyse Sam's reference ALS files
Build ALS template and Python patcher

sessions.summary: Three features: (1) pure-stdlib BPM auto-detection (Source/bpm_detector.py) borrowed from Automated DJ Mixes (attack_onsets + lattice_fit), no numpy/scipy/Demucs; bpm arg now optional, detects from kick; validated exact on 4 kicks. (2) Clip naming — track=display, clip=original filename. (3) Classifier patterns BVs/Tops/Fills/CABASA/DRM_/Ref Bounce. (4) Replaced duplicated-stems flat-reference GroupTrack with a single bounced flat-ref track (Source/bounce.py, pure-stdlib stem sum -> float32 WAV): color 37, muted, Ext. Out, last; supplied ref/master files kept as separate match tracks, never summed. Retired the ref-group-expand bug. (5) Working-track grouping: drums/music/vocals/fx with 2+ stems wrapped in a GroupTrack (audible, Main, expanded, category-coloured; children -> AudioOut/GroupTrack + TrackGroupId); kick/bass/sends standalone. (6) bounce.py numpy fast path (~15x) with bit-identical pure-stdlib fallback.
pending: Optional: classify INST ALL / instrumental bounces (currently land in music via fallback)
Optional: 'Bass' group when 2+ bass stems (currently off)
Optional: speed up find_audio_regions 24-bit RMS (slow part of large 24-bit builds)
Watch Python 3.14.0 interpreter glitches; stable 3.13 if builds become flaky

decisions.decision: ALS files are patched as raw text lines, not via XML libraries.
decisions.why: Ableton's parser is strict about whitespace, attribute order, and line endings. xml.etree.ElementTree corrupts the format. See ABLETON_INTERACTION.md.

decisions.decision: Stems are placed unwarped (IsWarped=false). BPM detected from kick/snare sets global tempo.
decisions.why: Sam's stems arrive pre-rendered at the correct BPM. No time-stretching needed.

decisions.decision: Flat reference is always our own bounce (sum of mix stems), never a supplied 'ref'/'riff'/master file. Single track at the bottom, color 14 (red), muted, routed to Ext. Out. Supplied references kept as separate match tracks (also red). No GroupTrack.
decisions.why: A supplied ref file can't be trusted to equal the stem sum (may be another artist's track or a limited master); verifying it would require bouncing the sum anyway, so always print our own. Single track + Ext. Out bypasses the master chain and retires the long-standing 'ref group opens expanded' bug.

decisions.decision: bounce.py uses numpy when installed (optional import) with a pure-stdlib fallback; both paths verified bit-identical. Working groups coloured with the category colour; group names Drums/Bass/Music/Vox/FX (bass IS grouped per Sam 2026-06-25). Reference tracks coloured red (14). Silence trim retuned tighter (headroom 55dB / window 0.1s / min-gap 2.5s / tail 1s) so clips hug audio without chopping decays.
decisions.why: Optional numpy keeps the tool install-free yet ~15x faster where numpy exists. Category-coloured group headers read at a glance. Sam asked for red refs, bass grouping, and tighter silence trimming after seeing the first demo.
